mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is in a sustained downtrend.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above policy comfort forces tighter Fed expectations and higher real discount rates, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the spread between triggered and untriggered mean returns is not negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, so equity earnings expectations deteriorate and S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise episodes fail to precede negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the signal reverses sign during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: An interaction between rising RSAFS and rising PAYEMS indicates broad nominal demand and payroll income growth, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent RSAFS and PAYEMS growth does not outperform either series alone, or if the joint-signal hit rate falls below 55% for 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and cyclical risk appetite, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month changes in INDPRO do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the rolling 5-year slope of the relationship turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below trend signals weakening housing demand and a broader slowdown in cyclical activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-breakdown episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the average post-trigger return is not materially below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is sticky while FEDFUNDS is already high, markets infer tighter-for-longer policy and a higher discount rate path, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is above 3.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-inflation/high-rate regime does not produce negative 5-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if the effect disappears outside tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in PAYEMS growth and rise in UNRATE identifies labor-market deterioration that reduces forward earnings visibility, making S&P 500 returns negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth turns negative while UNRATE rises at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-deterioration trigger fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if it adds no information beyond UNRATE alone.
